mnemonics: MACHINE LEARNING LINKS: Memory (AI) Logic (CS) Statistics (Math) Knowledge (DM) Learning (Neuro) Intelligence (Psychology) Neuroscience (Biology) Goals (Robotics)
keyPoints: Machine Learning is a subset of AI focused on algorithms that learn from data
It is heavily interdisciplinary, drawing core concepts from Computer Science, Statistics, and Mathematics
Its applications extend into numerous fields like Data Mining, Neuroscience, Psychology, Biology, and Robotics
commonMistakes: Believing ML is synonymous with AI (ML is a specific approach within AI)
Overlooking the foundational importance of statistics and probability theory
Assuming ML models can only be applied to their field of origin, ignoring their cross-disciplinary nature
